payload.message: GPT-5.6 proposes inputs; only the host executes them

title: First model counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 4500 cents → REPLACEMENT; inventory 10/0 → 9/1

counterexample.title: crossed-vip-damaged-4500
counterexample.rationale: Maximum among single crossed inputs: it directly separates tier-driven from amount-driven explanations by combining VIP with the exact amount previously observed only for STANDARD. It also minimizes redundancy and avoids confounding from condition or inventory changes.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crosses the observed VIP tier with the observed 4500-cent amount while holding DAMAGED condition, SKU, and inventory constant. A tier-driven explanation and an amount-driven explanation therefore imply different rule selection, without introducing an unobserved amount value.

title: High-information counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: high-value-vip-damaged-50000
counterexample.rationale: High: one input simultaneously probes the unresolved precedence of a high-value exception against the demonstrated VIP replacement rule and whether inventory mutation remains immediate or is deferred when that exception is reached. The 50000-cent amount is materially above both 4500 and 12000, while all other behavior-relevant observed fields remain controlled.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This keeps the already demonstrated VIP and DAMAGED predicates while raising the amount materially above the highest automatically processed observation. The resulting trace can reveal whether VIP replacement retains priority or a high-value exception supersedes automatic processing, while unchanged nonzero inventory makes immediate versus deferred inventory movement observable without adding an inventory confound.

title: Host narrowed the exact threshold
detail: 49999 cents processes automatically; 50000 cents enters review.

title: Host priority counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · priority at the boundary
counterexample.rationale: The host crosses customer tier at the exact review boundary to distinguish threshold priority from VIP replacement.

title: Host bounded the covered high-value interval
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

title: Contract rule · priority 1
detail: Priority 1: within the observed domain of DAMAGED returns for STANDARD and VIP customers, amountCents greater than or equal to 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no inventory mutation, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect. This rule runs before tier-specific rules; behavior for other conditions or tiers is unknown.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 1: within the observed domain of DAMAGED returns for STANDARD and VIP customers, amountCents greater than or equal to 50000 produces MANUAL_REVIEW, status PENDING_REVIEW, no inventory mutation, and a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect. This rule runs before tier-specific rules; behavior for other conditions or tiers is unknown.

title: Contract rule · priority 2
detail: Priority 2: after the high-value rule, a VIP, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, refundCents 0, sellable-minus-one, quarantine-plus-one, and a shipment side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 2: after the high-value rule, a VIP, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REPLACEMENT, status REPLACEMENT_ISSUED, refundCents 0, sellable-minus-one, quarantine-plus-one, and a shipment side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

title: Contract rule · priority 3
detail: Priority 3: after the high-value rule, a STANDARD, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine-plus-one, and a refund-ledger side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.

hypothesis.statement: Priority 3: after the high-value rule, a STANDARD, DAMAGED return with an observed valid amount below 50000 produces REFUND for the full amount, status REFUNDED, unchanged sellable inventory, quarantine-plus-one, and a refund-ledger side effect. Behavior for other conditions and unobserved amount validity domains is unknown.
